abstract.text: This article describes the KeyboardKit action engine.
Triggers the system keyboard switcher on release and long press.
This protocol can be implemented by any type that can be
used perform autocomplete as the user types.
This article describes the KeyboardKit autocomplete engine.
This article describes the KeyboardKit status engine.
This article describes the KeyboardKit device utilities.
This class provides a standard way to handle actions
that are triggered by the keyboard.
This namespace contains autocomplete-related types.
Keyboard-specific services.
The keyboard action handler to use.
This article describes the KeyboardKit preview engine.
This article describes the KeyboardKit app-specific utilities.
Handle a certain keyboard action gesture.
Inserts a space when released and can perform custom actions when long pressed.
This view mimics a native keyboard button.
This article describes the KeyboardKit proxy engine and its utilities.
This enum defines keyboard-specific actions, and is also
a namespace for keyboard action-related types.
Dismisses the keyboard when released.
Triggers when a button is double tapped.
This article describes the KeyboardKit callout engine.
This enum defines various keyboard gestures that can
be used to interact with a keyboard.
The standard controller action.
This article describes the KeyboardKit feedback engine.
Triggers a locale switcher action on release and menu on long press.
The standard button image for the action.
This article describes the KeyboardKit navigation engine.
The standard button text for the action.
This article describes the KeyboardKit external keyboard engine.
Changes the keyboard type when pressed.
This article describes the KeyboardKit gesture engine.
This article describes the KeyboardKit styling engine.
KeyboardKit helps you create custom keyboard extensions with Swift and SwiftUI.
This view can be used to mimic a native iOS keyboard.
Deletes backwards when pressed, and repeats until released.
This article describes the KeyboardKit text input engine, which lets you type within the keyboard extension.
This article describes the KeyboardKit localization engine.
The image to which the action refers, if any.
This article describes how KeyboardKit Pro can identify the host application.
This article describes the KeyboardKit emoji engine.
This article describes the KeyboardKit AI support capabilities.

handle triggered
events.
A diacritic action, that will replace any previous match when release.

Inserts a text character when released.
This article describes the KeyboardKit Pro theme engine, which can be used to style the entire keyboard with a single theme.
This article describes the essential parts of KeyboardKit.
This article describes the KeyboardKit settings engine.
This namespace contains essential keyboard-related types.
This toolbar can be added above the keyboard to show
autocomplete suggestions as the user types.
Inserts an emoji when released.
This article describes the KeyboardKit layout engine.
This article describes the KeyboardKit dictation engine.
Represents a command (⌘) key.
